name: Tour of the Four Places
name_original: Monroy por Ermita Santa Ana – circular desde Santiago del Campo
description: Explore the Monroy por Ermita Santa Ana circular mountain biking route from Santiago del Campo, a moderate 41.2-mile trail.
region_intro: Mountain bike trails around Santiago Del Campo offer a variety of routes across a generally undulating landscape. The region features trails with moderate elevation gains, suggesting a mix of flatter sections and gentle climbs. The routes often traverse open areas and can include features like reservoirs and pine forests. This terrain provides accessible mountain biking experiences for various skill levels. Best mountain bike trails around Santiago Del Campo The most popular mountain bike trail is Circunvalación del Pantano del Casar Trail, an easy 11.5 miles (18.5 km) trail that takes 1 hour 22 minutes to complete. This route features a low elevation gain of under 100 meters. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Casar Reservoir Loop, a moderate 22.5 miles (36.2 km) path. This route offers views around the Casar Reservoir and includes over 230 meters of ascent. Local mountain bikers also love the Return of Pinares de Garrovillas from Garrovillas de Alconétar, a 13.5 miles (21.8 km) trail leading through pine forests, often completed in about 1 hour 55 minutes. Mountain biking around Santiago Del Campo is defined by reservoir loops, pine forests, and routes with moderate elevation changes. The network offers options for easy to difficult ability levels, with a majority of moderate trails. The routes in Santiago Del Campo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.2 stars from more than 10 reviews. More than 100 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Santiago Del Campo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available in Santiago Del Campo?
A: Santiago Del Campo offers a selection of 13 mountain bike trails. These routes cater to various skill levels, including 1 easy, 11 moderate, and 1 difficult trail, ensuring options for most riders.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Santiago Del Campo?
A: The terrain in Santiago Del Campo is generally undulating, featuring a mix of flatter sections and gentle climbs. You'll often find routes traversing open areas, alongside reservoirs, and through pine forests. The landscape provides accessible mountain biking experiences without extreme elevation changes.

Q: Are there any family-friendly mountain bike trails in Santiago Del Campo?
A: Yes, there is an easy mountain bike trail suitable for families. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1014464453/circunvalacion-del-pantano-del-casar-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Circunvalación del Pantano del Casar Trail&lt;/a&gt; is 11.5 miles (18.5 km) long with a low elevation gain, making it a great option for a relaxed family ride.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in Santiago Del Campo?
A: Many of the trails in Santiago Del Campo are designed as loops, offering convenient circular routes. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1016754603/casar-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Casar Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a popular moderate 22.5-mile (36.2 km) path that circles the reservoir, providing scenic views.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Santiago Del Campo?
A: The mountain bike trails in Santiago Del Campo are highly regarded by the komoot community, holding an average rating of 4.2 stars from over 10 reviews. Riders often appreciate the varied terrain, including reservoir loops and pine forests, and the accessibility for different skill levels.

Q: Are there any longer mountain bike routes for experienced riders?
A: Yes, for those seeking a longer challenge, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1014744243/tour-of-the-four-places" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tour of the Four Places&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 41.2-mile (66.3 km) route with over 370 meters of ascent, offering a more extensive ride through the region.

Q: What interesting sights can I see near the mountain bike trails in Santiago Del Campo?
A: While mountain biking, you can explore several interesting attractions. Consider visiting the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4085465" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bridge Over the Tagus Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3827014" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of Santiago&lt;/a&gt;, or the unique &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2720800" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cheese Museum - Torta del Casar&lt;/a&gt;. There are also several viewpoints offering stunning vistas of the Alcántara Reservoir.

Q: Are there any trails that go through pine forests?
A: Yes, the region is known for its pine forests. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1014343997/return-of-pinares-de-garrovillas-from-garrovillas-de-alconetar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Return of Pinares de Garrovillas from Garrovillas de Alconétar&lt;/a&gt; is a 13.5-mile (21.8 km) trail that leads directly through these scenic pine forests.

Q: What is the most challenging mountain bike trail in Santiago Del Campo?
A: There is one difficult-grade mountain bike trail available in Santiago Del Campo for riders looking for a significant challenge. This route offers the most demanding experience in the area.

Q: Are there any trails that feature reservoirs?
A: Yes, reservoirs are a prominent feature of the landscape. Many routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1016754603/casar-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Casar Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt;, offer views and paths around these bodies of water, providing a refreshing element to your ride.

Q: What is the average elevation gain on trails in Santiago Del Campo?
A: The trails in Santiago Del Campo generally feature moderate elevation gains. While some routes have less than 100 meters of ascent, others like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1016754603/casar-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Casar Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt; include over 230 meters, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1014343997/return-of-pinares-de-garrovillas-from-garrovillas-de-alconetar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Return of Pinares de Garrovillas&lt;/a&gt; has over 300 meters, offering a good mix of climbs.
tips:
The Talaván Reservoir is one of the Protected Areas of Extremadura.​
Some of the bird species that can be found are kites, cormorants, cattle egrets, bustards, canasteras, seagulls or storks. The most impressive is the crane that arrives in large numbers in November. So if you're going for a hike or mountain bike, bring your binoculars.
The current building dates from the 19th century, but the existence of a hermitage here is known in documents from the 17th century.
The origins of the fortress date back to the year 1309. The Monroy family was very important in Extremadura at the time, politically and militarily. If you are in the area it is worth a visit.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the Monroy por Ermita Santa Ana mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: This circular mountain bike route is approximately 66 kilometers long and typically takes around 5 hours to complete, depending on your pace and stops.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this mountain bike trail?
faqs.answer: The terrain is varied, offering a mix of natural beauty and historical elements. You can expect to traverse paths through Mediterranean forests, dehesas (wooded pastures), olive groves, holm oak groves, and pine forests. While much of the route is moderate, some sections, particularly near the Ermita de Santa Ana, may feature more abrupt and steep terrain.

faqs.question: What landmarks and viewpoints can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: The route offers several points of interest. You will pass by the Talaván Reservoir, the historic Ermita de Santa Ana, and the impressive Monroy Castle. The area also features centenarian fountains, ponds, streams, monumental remains, and archaeological sites, providing excellent opportunities for birdwatching.

faqs.question: Is the Monroy por Ermita Santa Ana mountain bike trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: The trail is rated as moderate in difficulty. While it offers diverse landscapes, some sections, especially around the Ermita de Santa Ana, can be abrupt and steep. Beginners with some mountain biking experience and good fitness might manage, but those new to the sport may find it challenging. There are shorter, easier routes in the broader Monroy area if you're looking for a less demanding ride.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trail from Santiago del Campo?
faqs.answer: As the trail starts and ends in Santiago del Campo, you can typically find parking within or on the outskirts of the village. Specific designated parking for this exact route is not detailed, but local parking options in Santiago del Campo should be available.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this route?
faqs.answer: The region of Extremadura, where this trail is located, experiences hot summers. Therefore, the best times to mountain bike are during the spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November). During these seasons, the temperatures are milder, and the natural landscapes are at their most vibrant.

faqs.question: Is this mountain bike route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While specific regulations for this exact route are not provided, many trails in the Cáceres region are dog-friendly. It is generally recommended to keep dogs on a leash, especially due to the presence of wildlife and potential livestock in dehesa areas. Ensure your dog is fit enough for the distance and varied terrain.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to access and ride the Monroy por Ermita Santa Ana circular mountain bike trail. It is a public route through natural and historical landscapes.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife and flora can I expect to see?
faqs.answer: The area is rich in biodiversity. You might spot various bird species, including black vultures, griffon vultures, black storks, and eagle owls, especially in the rugged terrain near the Ermita de Santa Ana. The flora includes Mediterranean forests, dehesas with holm oaks, olive groves, and pine forests.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or pubs near the trail for refreshments?
faqs.answer: As the route starts and ends in Santiago del Campo, you may find local establishments there for refreshments. Monroy, another village along the route, also offers options for food and drink. It's always a good idea to carry sufficient water and snacks, especially for a route of this length.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route overlap with the Camino de Las Quebradas and the Camino de las Qubradas.
